_meta.purpose: Maps fields confirmed in Skyward's Standards Gradebook Teacher Guide (Class Roster and Student Information reports) to Dean's canonical concepts. Built from the teacher-facing UI manual, not a real data export -- verify every row against an actual anonymized export file before trusting it in production.

status_meanings.mapped: canonical field exists in core/schema.py and/or knowledge/synonyms.json
status_meanings.needs_join: field is per-course or per-record (schedule, discipline, academic history) -- a flat one-row-per-student sheet cannot represent it without a join or a sibling sheet
status_meanings.unresolved: not yet given a canonical mapping; low analytical value or too shape-dependent to model blind

class_roster_report.skyward_field: Student ID / Internal ID / Name Key

class_roster_report.note: no analytic use case yet; add only if a real question needs it
added in this pass; sensitive by default via core/privacy.py contact keywords

class_roster_report.skyward_field: Primary Guardian / All Guardians (name, phone, email, address)

student_information_report.skyward_field: Attendance Totals (Excused/Unexcused/Tardy/Other, by term or date range)
student_information_report.note: Skyward tracks 4 distinct absence categories, not one rate -- confirm the real export's column granularity before assuming a single 'Attendance Rate' number exists

CRITICAL: this is one-to-many (student -> multiple courses -> each with its own teacher). A flat roster sheet cannot answer 'which teacher is performing well' unless a schedule/grades sheet keyed by student+course is also present and joinable. Confirm with counselor whether their export includes this or only the roster-level Class Roster fields.
per-course, same join dependency as Current Schedule

distinct from the 'discipline' concept (= academic department/field of study) used by College/University question-library entries -- see conduct_status entry in knowledge/synonyms.json. Real column header will likely be literally 'Discipline Information'; core/privacy.py now flags that as sensitive (previously it would have slipped through unredacted).
multi-year, per-course -- out of scope for a single current-term roster sheet

student_information_report.skyward_field: Current Grades (per course, + Teacher Comments + Cumulative GPA)

student_information_report.canonical: sat_math, sat_ebrw, sat_total, psat_math, psat_reading_writing, psat_total, benchmark fields
student_information_report.note: already covered by existing PSAT/SAT canonical fields; confirm the real export's column labels match (e.g. 'SAT Math' vs 'SAT - Math Score')
not a data field a counselor would export for analysis
added in this pass; sensitive by default via core/privacy.py contact keywords
binary/image data, not relevant to a query planner
should never appear in an analytic export; flag as sensitive if it ever does

open_questions_for_real_export: Is the export one flat sheet (roster-level, one row per student) or does it include a schedule/grades sheet keyed by student+course? This determines whether 'which teacher is performing well' is answerable at all.
Is Attendance a single rate/percentage column, or the four raw category counts (Excused/Unexcused/Tardy/Other) that Skyward actually tracks?
What are the literal column header strings? (e.g. 'GPA' vs 'Current Cumulative GPA' vs 'Cum GPA') -- core/schema.py's phrase matching needs the real headers to validate against, not guessed ones.
Does the export include Discipline Information at all, or is that pulled separately (it's a security-gated report in Skyward)?
